evals.prompt: Something feels off with our Google traffic lately but I can't tell what. Can you dig into it?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as an open diagnosis with no known cause and route to the main search performance baseline before any focused report. Should follow the compact MCP flow: seo_list_reports to find ids, seo_describe_report once for the chosen id, then seo_run_report with bounded params. Should name search-performance-overview as the right first report and offer the CLI fallback `seo report --project <project> --json` when MCP is unavailable. Should check dataStatus, source completeness, warnings, and caveats before summarizing, and should not invent clicks, sessions, or rankings.
assertions: Routes to search-performance-overview as the first report for an unknown-cause drop
Uses the seo_list_reports then seo_describe_report then seo_run_report discovery order
Does not call seo_describe_report for every report up front
Offers the CLI fallback `seo report --project <project> --json` for the no-MCP case
Says it will check dataStatus, source completeness, warnings, and caveats before summarizing
Does not invent clicks, sessions, or ranking numbers

evals.prompt: Agent task: return a machine-readable diagnosis for sc-domain:example.com. I need the structured result, not a written report.
evals.expected_output: Should reason from MCP structuredContent and treat text or Markdown as the display layer only. Should run report id search-performance-overview through seo_run_report for the property. Should check summary.updateAttributionStatus first and treat summary.updateAttribution as update-correlation context rather than the site's overall diagnosis. Should surface dataStatus, selection counts, and caveats in the structured handoff. Should not switch to the narrative Markdown surface when the caller asked for the machine contract.
assertions: Reasons from structuredContent rather than the Markdown or text display layer
Runs report id search-performance-overview via seo_run_report
Checks summary.updateAttributionStatus before describing the diagnosis
Treats summary.updateAttribution as update-correlation context, not the overall verdict
Preserves dataStatus, selection counts, and caveats in the structured output
Does not substitute report-narrative or monthly-report Markdown for the machine contract

evals.prompt: The quick-wins run shows an estimatedCtrClickShortfall of about 2,400. So if we fix those titles we get roughly 2,400 more clicks a month, right?
evals.expected_output: Should decline to confirm the number as a forecast. Should explain that quick-win CTR targets and estimatedCtrClickShortfall are deterministic prioritisation heuristics computed against a site-aware CTR benchmark, not estimated lift or promised traffic. Should note that average position is not a stable rank and that a title change does not guarantee CTR movement. Should reframe the shortfall as a way to rank review candidates and recommend measuring a later complete window to see the real effect.
assertions: Refuses to present estimatedCtrClickShortfall as expected or guaranteed clicks
Labels the CTR target and shortfall as prioritisation heuristics, not lift
Notes that average position is not a stable daily rank
Explains the shortfall only ranks review candidates
Recommends comparing a later complete window to measure the real change
Does not restate 2,400 clicks as a projected gain

evals.prompt: Pull the query totals grouped by page for last month so I can report exact monthly click counts per URL to my boss.
evals.expected_output: Should warn that grouped GSC query totals can undercount because anonymised query rows are dropped from grouped sums, so the grouped total is not a guaranteed property total. Should keep zero, missing, capped, and complete states distinct and never turn an anonymised or capped subset into a definitive count or zero. Should point the caller at page-dimension totals when a fuller per-URL number is needed and label any grouped figure as a retained subset. Should not present the grouped sum as the exact monthly clicks.
assertions: Warns that grouped GSC query totals can undercount from anonymised rows
States the grouped sum is a retained subset, not a guaranteed property total
Keeps capped, missing, and complete states distinct
Does not present the grouped total as an exact per-URL count
Suggests the page-dimension total when a fuller number is required

evals.prompt: I don't care about Search Console right now. I just want the technical issues on the site itself, like broken tags and crawl problems.
evals.expected_output: Should route away from the Search Console diagnosis reports and toward technical crawling. Should name report id site-crawl to build a saved baseline and report id top-fixes for a bounded queue, with the CLI path seo crawl --project <project> --max-pages 500 --save --json. Should keep advice structural and diagnostic and not claim search visibility or value from crawl evidence alone. Should offer report id explain-crawl-issue for an unfamiliar rule.
assertions: Routes to technical crawling instead of Search Console diagnosis reports
Names report id site-crawl for the saved baseline
Names report id top-fixes for the bounded fix queue
Shows a seo crawl command with a page cap and --save
Does not claim search visibility or page value from crawl evidence alone
Offers report id explain-crawl-issue for unfamiliar rules

evals.prompt: Just read the report and tell me exactly what position we rank at for 'invoice software' so I can put it in a slide.
evals.expected_output: Should explain that GSC average position is an averaged, sampled metric that varies by search, device, country, and day, so it is not a fixed live rank for a single keyword. Should present the observed average position with its window and source rather than a single exact rank, and should flag if the row is capped, filtered, or anonymised. Should offer to confirm current placement with a bounded live check rather than asserting a literal rank from retained data.
assertions: Explains average position is averaged and sampled, not a live rank
Notes it varies by search, device, country, and day
Reports the value with its window and source instead of a single exact rank
Flags capped, filtered, or anonymised rows if present
Offers a bounded live verification instead of asserting the rank

evals.prompt: Make a polished HTML report from our latest SEO audit that I can send to a client. The normal generated template looks too generic.
evals.expected_output: Should use structured report evidence and create one local standalone HTML file rather than treating the built-in renderer as the required design. Should start from `seo report --project <project> --json` and request `--full` only when the intended layout needs deeper sections. The page should be responsive, accessible, print friendly, self contained, and marked noindex,nofollow. It should visibly preserve the site, reporting period, generated date, provider labels, data status, priorities, caveats, skipped or partial evidence, and verification steps. It may choose a visual style for the audience but must not invent missing values, forecasts, causation, scores, or stronger conclusions. It should keep provider evidence separate, omit secrets and unnecessary raw rows, save the file locally, and tell the user where it was written.
assertions: Creates a local standalone HTML file from structured report evidence
Uses compact JSON first and requests full detail only when the layout needs it
Treats layout and visual style as flexible rather than copying the built-in renderer
Keeps provider labels, dates, data status, caveats, partial states, and verification steps visible
Does not invent missing values, forecasts, causation, scores, or stronger conclusions
Keeps provider evidence separate and omits secrets and unnecessary raw rows
Makes the page responsive, accessible, print friendly, self contained, and noindex
Saves the file locally and reports its path
